mappings.rationaleAI: Traces the historical development of medical education, hospital practice, the Hippocratic tradition, and public health (A, C, D).
Directly addresses the ethical basis of medical practice including euthanasia, consent, and health policy (C.2.a).
Examines licensure, legal restrictions on practice, consent, confidentiality, and organ donation regulation (C.2.b, C.2.c).
A core text on public health services, disease prevention, sanitation, and population-level health administration (D, F, H).
Essential for understanding disease surveillance, population health data, and the scientific methods underpinning epidemic prevention and control (H.5).
Addresses vaccination, quarantine, antibiotic use, and vector control strategies for prevention and control of infection (H).
Examines epidemic prevention and control including quarantine, isolation, and public health responses to outbreaks (H.2, H.5).
Illustrates historical quarantine measures and public health responses to epidemic diseases (H.2, H.5).
Global prevention programs, public health policy, and coordinated treatment strategies for HIV/AIDS illuminate modern epidemic management (H.5).
Vaccine technology and virus infection control are central topics, directly relevant to vaccination and immunization programs (H.1).
Antibiotic resistance and aseptic practices are key themes, relevant to destruction of infectious agents and prophylactic medication (H.3, H.4).
Explores drug regulation and prescription practices relevant to control of drugs and therapeutic agent use (H.4, I.2).
Nutrient requirements, obesity, and undernutrition are directly relevant to prevention of malnutrition (G).
Food safety, food poisoning, and genetically modified food are examined in the context of food supply control (G, I.2).
A key field of specialized hospital and specialist practice essential to surgical care (B, C.1.b).
Explores psychiatry as a specialized field of medical practice with hospital, community, and research dimensions (B, C.1.b).
The National Health Service and social services are central topics, relevant to governmental public health practice and health legislation (C.1.c, D, I.2).
Zoonotic disease control and vaccination history -- including Edward Jenner's contributions -- are relevant to prevention of cross-species infection (H).
Environmental protection legislation directly bears on the control of air, water, and soil pollution (F, I.2).
Pollution control policy and environmental regulation inform debates about environmental sanitation and health (F).
Population dynamics, life expectancy, and demographic transitions are essential to world population and public health planning (D, G).
Explores laws, regulations, and treatment programs for substance use relevant to drug control and industrial medicine (H.4, I).
Forensic examination and evidence bear on the legal aspects of medical practice and cause-of-death determination (C.2.c).
Community health, disability, and mental health social services are central themes, relevant to public health services and social medicine (D, I).
Educational systems and teaching methods inform medical education and professional training (A).
